This unique belief system, serving as a tangible manifestation of the Malay concept of divine kingship, will be meticulously analyzed. This research dissected rituals, symbols, and underlying philosophies associated with the devarāja cult, drawing comparisons and contrasts with the European model of divine right. title: Divine right of kings | Definition, History, & Facts - Britannica
highlights: political doctrine in defense of monarchical absolutism, which asserted that kings derived their authority from God and could not therefore be held accountable for  ...    ruler, paralleling the award of spiritual power to the church. By the 16th and 17th centuries, however, the new national monarchs were asserting their authority in matters of both church and state. King James I of England (reigned 1603–25) was the foremost exponent of the divine right of kings, but the doctrine virtually disappeared from English politics after the Glorious Revolution(1688–89). In the late 17th and 18th centuries, kings such as Louis XIV(1643–1715) of France continued to profit from the divine-right theory, even though many of them no longer had any truly religious belief in it. title: Louis XIV and the Power of France - Exploros
highlights: As an adult, he took full control of the government and made it clear that he believed in absolutism, a system where the king held all power. Louis was known as the “Sun King” because he saw himself as the center of France, just as the sun is the center of the universe. He famously said, “I am the state,” meaning that all government decisions came from him alone. The Divine Right of Kings Louis XIV believed in the divine right of kings—the idea that his authority to rule came directly from God. He claimed that he alone was chosen by God to lead France, and that disobeying him was not just a political act, but a sin against divine order. But Louis didn’t just believe this—he made sure everyone else believed it too. He used art, ceremony, and court life to reinforce his power. At court, everything revolved around the king.
